pattern: Every operation always changes the gap between the two numbers by exactly two in the most favorable direction, so the widest closeable gap is a direct multiple of how many operations are allowed.
hints: Look at the difference between x and num rather than the two numbers themselves -- each operation can only ever shrink or grow that difference by exactly two.
To keep x as large as possible while still being able to close the gap, every operation should decrease x by one and increase num by one at the same time.
The widest gap that t such operations can close is exactly 2 * t, so the largest starting x is num plus that amount.

edge_inputs.description: smallest num with the largest number of operations
largest num with the smallest number of operations
num and t equal to each other

stress.description: random num and t across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num = rng.randint(1, 50)
    t = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [num, t]
